LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.EnableDesc: If disabled, the gravship raid incident will never fire and will not be offered as a raid option.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.OdysseyMissing: The Odyssey DLC was not detected. Gravship raids require Odyssey and will remain inactive until it is enabled.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.MinColonistCount: Minimum free colonists required on the map
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.EnableRaidshipEff: Real thruster/dust effects on enemy gravships

Enemy gravships show real thruster fire, exhaust, and ground dust while landing and departing, instead of a plain silhouette. Does not take control of the camera or hide the UI. Disable if this new rendering code causes visual issues on your system.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.AllowEnemyGravcor: Enemy gravship cores yield a gravcore

If enabled, destroying or deconstructing a landed enemy gravship's control core yields one vanilla gravcore, exactly like destroying a grav engine. Disabled by default, since a gravcore is a major progression reward.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.HardcoreEnemyDepa: Hardcore: enemy gravships destroy unanchored maps when leaving

If enabled, an enemy gravship that successfully departs from a map with no grav anchor present will abandon and remove that map entirely, exactly like a player gravship taking off from an unanchored map. If you have no surviving free colonists anywhere else when this happens, the game ends immediately with closing credits and returns you to the main menu. Disabled by default: this is a major, irreversible gameplay consequence.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.EnableGravshipGua: If enabled, a share of each newly generated gravship raid stays behind to defend the landed ship instead of assaulting the colony. Guards defend the ship's hull and core, and board with the rest of the crew once the raid retreats. Only applies to raids created after this option is changed.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.EnableMinPlayerTe: Require a minimum colony tech level

If enabled, gravship raids will not occur until your faction's tech level is at least the level chosen below. Useful to keep gravships away from low-tech starts (e.g. tribal) until your colony has caught up.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.DebugLoggingDesc: Logs extra diagnostic messages about gravship raid selection, landing, and lord behavior to the console.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Settings.EnableGlobalFacti: If enabled, factions excluded below can never be a gravship raid source, regardless of what any individual raid template's own allowed/disallowed faction lists say. Disabled by default.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersGivenUpRetre: The {0} of {1} have given up the attack and are falling back to their gravship.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersSatisfiedRet: The {0} of {1} are satisfied with the damage they've done and are falling back to their gravship.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersRetreatingCa: The {0} of {1} have taken too many casualties and are falling back to their gravship.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersRetreatingNo: The {0} of {1} are no longer hostile and are falling back to their gravship.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersBoarding: Surviving {0} of {1} are boarding their gravship.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageRaidersStranded: The {0} of {1} have lost their gravship and are fleeing on foot.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.MessageGravshipCoreDestroy: The gravship of the {0} of {1} has been destroyed, stranding any survivors.
LanguageData.GravshipRaids.Ending.UnguardedMapDestroy: Your colony had no grav anchor to hold its ground against a departing enemy gravship.
As the raiders' gravship tore away, it took the map, and everyone left on it, with it. No colonists remain to carry on.
